title: Diagnose a 500 error after deploying a FastAPI app to cloud
description: A FastAPI app works locally but returns HTTP 500 after cloud deployment.
prompt: Give a practical debugging plan for a 500 error after deployment. Include likely causes, logs to inspect, and a safe remediation path.
constraints: Do not guess a single exact root cause.
Mention rollback or safe deployment if needed.

strong_answer_includes: Inspect application logs and platform logs
Check environment variables, secrets, database connectivity, and startup command
Reproduce with a minimal request and correlation time
Rollback or disable risky change if users are impacted

sample_answer: A 500 means the server hit an unhandled error, so I would start with the cloud app logs around the failing request time rather than guessing. I would check environment variables, secrets, connection strings, database/network access, package versions, and the startup command because those often differ from local. I would also hit a simple health endpoint to separate startup issues from route-specific bugs. If users are affected, I would roll back to the last working deployment or temporarily disable the failing feature while fixing the root cause.
canonical_solution_summary: Use logs, config comparison, dependency checks, and safe rollback to diagnose a deployed 500.
